data.title: Anchorage Digital, Kamino, and Solana Company announce joint institutional capital venture
data.description:
Anchorage Digital, Kamino and Solana Company have announced a collaboration that has been described as a first-of-its-kind tri-party custody model. It unlocks an efficient strategy for onchain borrowing on Solana without moving assets out of Anchorage Digital Bank, which means that staked SOL can now be used as collateral for loans within a regulated environment. […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/anchorage-digital-kamino-solana-company-jv/

data.title: Grayscale Seeks to Convert AAVE Trust into ETF
data.description: Scott Melker, host of The Wolf Of All Streets Podcast, posted on X about Grayscale's recent filing. Grayscale aims to convert its $AAVE Trust into an ETF. This move is part of Grayscale's strategy to expand its offerings in the crypto market. The conversion process is subject to regulatory approval and could impact the accessibility of AAVE investments for institutional investors. Grayscale's initiative reflects the growing interest in integrating crypto assets into traditional financial products.

data.title: Mavis Tire Considers U.S. Initial Public Offering
data.description: Mavis Tire, known for its brands such as Midas and Tuffy, is reportedly exploring the possibility of launching an initial public offering (IPO) in the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, citing unnamed sources familiar with the matter, that the company is evaluating the potential benefits of going public. The move could position Mavis Tire to expand its market presence and capitalize on investor interest. Details regarding the timing or size of the IPO have not been disclosed, and the company has yet to make an official announcement. The decision to pursue an IPO would align with Mavis Tire's strategic goals of growth and increased visibility in the automotive service industry.

data.title: Nathan Chen Reflects on Olympic Journey from Fall to Gold
data.description: American figure skater Nathan Chen, known as the "Quad King," experienced a significant setback during the 2018 Winter Olympics when he fell during his routine. New York Times posted on X, highlighting Chen's journey from this disappointment to triumph. Four years later, Chen achieved redemption by winning a gold medal, showcasing his resilience and determination in the sport.

data.title: Senator Kelly Anticipates Continued Criticism from Trump and Hegseth
data.description: Senator Mark Kelly has expressed his expectation that U.S. President Donald Trump and Pete Hegseth will persist in their criticism of him and other Democratic lawmakers. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Kelly's remarks regarding the ongoing scrutiny over a video involving Democratic lawmakers allegedly disobeying orders. Kelly's comments come amid heightened political tensions and partisan disagreements. He emphasized the importance of addressing these criticisms while continuing to focus on legislative priorities. The senator's statement reflects the broader political climate, where both parties are actively engaging in public discourse over various issues.

data.title: Norway Leads Medal Count at 2026 Winter Olympics After Day 7
data.description: Norway has reclaimed the top position in the medal standings at the 2026 Winter Olympics, surpassing Italy after the seventh day of competition. New York Times posted on X, highlighting that the United States follows closely behind in the rankings. The competition continues to be fierce as countries vie for Olympic glory in various winter sports disciplines. The updated standings reflect the ongoing efforts and achievements of athletes from around the world as they compete in this prestigious international event.

data.title: Malaysia's Anti-Corruption Commission Faces Allegations Amid Government Crisis
data.description: Malaysia's Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is under scrutiny following allegations of corruption within the agency itself. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the irony of the situation as the MACC is responsible for enforcing accountability and transparency. Despite the agency's denial of any misconduct, the controversy raises concerns about a potential deeper crisis within the Malaysian government. The situation has sparked discussions about the effectiveness and integrity of the country's anti-corruption efforts, with observers questioning the impact on public trust and governance.

data.title: Investors Shift from US Crypto ETFs to International Equity ETFs Amid Rising Treasury Yields
data.description: US Spot Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs are witnessing continuous outflows as investors redirect their capital towards international equity ETFs. According to NS3.AI, this shift is influenced by increasing US Treasury yields and robust labor market data, prompting a move away from high-beta crypto assets to more secure or appealing overseas markets. While this trend is affecting short-term liquidity for crypto assets, it does not compromise the long-term investment potential for Bitcoin and Ethereum.

data.title: Pi Network Enters Final Infrastructure Phase as Node Deadline Approaches
data.description:
Pi Network’s long-awaited infrastructure upgrade has entered its final stage in February 2026, bringing firm deadlines for node operators and measurable progress on Mainnet migration. The transition marks one of the most technically significant periods in the project’s development cycle.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/pi-network-enters-final-infrastructure-phase-as-node-deadline-approaches/

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Keir Starmer Advocates Closer Ties with EU and Reduced Dependence on U.S. Military Support
data.description: Keir Starmer, leader of the Labour Party, is set to change his stance in Munich by advocating for stronger ties between Britain and the European Union. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Starmer's intention to reduce Britain's reliance on American military support. This marks a significant shift from his previous position of maintaining a balanced approach between Washington and Brussels. Starmer's speech is expected to emphasize the importance of European collaboration in defense and security matters, aiming to foster a more independent and robust European defense strategy. His remarks come amid ongoing discussions about the future of Britain's foreign policy and its role in global security dynamics.

data.title: Ford Discusses Potential Partnerships for Chinese Automakers in the U.S.
data.description: Ford's top executive has engaged in discussions with officials from the Trump administration regarding a possible strategy for Chinese automakers to manufacture vehicles in the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that these talks focus on forming partnerships between Chinese and American car companies. This initiative aims to facilitate the entry of Chinese automotive firms into the U.S. market by collaborating with established domestic manufacturers. The discussions are part of broader efforts to enhance cooperation and explore new opportunities within the automotive industry.

data.title: Kazakhstan's Mikhail Shaidorov Secures Historic Gold in Men's Figure Skating
data.description: Kazakhstan's Mikhail Shaidorov delivered an extraordinary performance in men's individual figure skating, earning a gold medal at the Winter Olympics. New York Times posted on X, highlighting the significance of this achievement as it marks only the second time Kazakhstan has won a gold medal in any Winter Olympics event. Shaidorov's victory is a momentous occasion for the nation, showcasing his exceptional talent and dedication to the sport.

data.title: Impact of Tech CEOs' Race for Artificial General Intelligence on Consumers and Developers
data.description:
Fred Hickey, editor of The High-Tech Strategist, posted on X about the ongoing competition among tech CEOs to achieve artificial general intelligence (AGI). This pursuit has led to significant shortages in electronic components, particularly semiconductor memories. As these CEOs invest heavily in their quest, the average American consumer faces rising energy costs and increased prices for electronic devices such as PCs, smartphones, and TVs. Additionally, there are concerns about potential shortages of these products.

While the race for AGI promises advancements like improved search capabilities and faster coding for software developers, who constitute less than 0.4% of the U.S. population, it also raises fears of job losses. Despite ambitious claims of solving major global issues like cancer and achieving world peace, Hickey suggests that the public might benefit more if these CEOs focused on less consequential challenges, such as the proposed but unrealized UFC-style cage matches between Elon Musk and Mark Zuckerberg.

data.title: AI Boom Faces Challenges Amid Changing Dynamics
data.description: Bespoke Investment Group posted on X that the latest report titled "AI Boom to AI Doom" explores the evolving landscape of artificial intelligence. The report, spanning 38 pages, delves into the challenges and opportunities facing the AI sector. As the industry experiences rapid growth, concerns about sustainability and potential setbacks are emerging. The report highlights key factors influencing the trajectory of AI development and its impact on various sectors. Industry experts are closely monitoring these developments to assess future implications. The report aims to provide insights into the shifting dynamics within the AI industry.

data.title: New York Scammers Allegedly Steal $120,000,000 From Medicare and Medicaid, Paying Elderly Patients To Fuel Scheme
data.description:
Two men from New York are accused of running a healthcare scheme that defrauded Medicare and Medicaid out of more than $100 million. The U. S. Department of Justice (DOJ) says it’s charging two men from Queens with masterminding a scam that stole $120 million from Medicare and Medicaid by submitting bogus claims
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2026/02/13/new-york-scammers-allegedly-steal-120000000-from-medicare-and-medicaid-paying-elderly-patients-to-fuel-scheme/

data.title: Canada Advances Carbon Capture in Oil Sands Amid U.S. Policy Shift
data.description: Canada's energy minister announced the government's commitment to developing carbon capture technology in the Alberta oil sands. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this initiative aims to ensure the industry adapts to environmental changes despite the U.S. reversing its environmental policies. The Canadian government is focused on maintaining progress in sustainable practices within the energy sector, emphasizing the importance of innovation in reducing carbon emissions. This move comes as the U.S. alters its approach to environmental regulations, potentially impacting global efforts to combat climate change. The development of carbon capture technology is seen as a crucial step in aligning the oil sands industry with evolving environmental standards.

data.title: Hong Kong ADRs Show Mixed Performance Compared to Local Market
data.description: Hong Kong's American Depositary Receipts (ADRs) exhibited varied movements in comparison to the local market closing figures. According to RTHK, HSBC and Alibaba ADRs fell by nearly 2% or more compared to their Hong Kong closing prices. In contrast, Meituan's ADRs rose by almost 2%.

data.title: Wall Street Faces Challenges as Consensus Trades Falter in 2026
data.description: Wall Street began 2026 with high confidence, marked by record-low cash reserves, minimal hedging, and strong conviction in their strategies. Bloomberg posted on X that just six weeks into the year, many of these widely held trades are not performing as expected. The financial sector is now grappling with the repercussions of these missteps, as investors reassess their positions and strategies in light of the unexpected market dynamics. The situation highlights the inherent risks of consensus trading and the need for adaptability in the ever-changing financial landscape.
